clinical_coherence.notes: 48M with new-onset seizure and worsening headaches for 3 weeks. Recent dental abscess with extraction 6 weeks ago creating brain abscess differential. Remote history of optic neuritis at age 31 with inconclusive MS workup adds a demyelinating disease differential (tumefactive MS). PMH includes HTN, DM2, penicillin allergy. Well-layered clinical complexity.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI shows ring-enhancing mass. The dental history and low-grade fever raise abscess concern. History of optic neuritis raises tumefactive MS consideration. Advanced imaging (perfusion, spectroscopy, DWI patterns) should help differentiate. Pathology confirms GBM. All internally consistent.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD C71.9 correct. Differential includes brain abscess (dental source) and tumefactive MS (prior optic neuritis) alongside the correct GBM diagnosis. Both are clinically plausible differentials.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Excellent puzzle with TWO confounds: (1) dental abscess raising brain abscess concern, and (2) prior optic neuritis raising tumefactive MS concern. Both are realistic and require careful evaluation to exclude. More complex than P01 which had only the abscess confound.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 48M with HTN, DM2, prior optic neuritis at 31. Remote ON with negative MS workup but no subsequent events is plausible. Penicillin allergy adds medication constraint realism.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding case with dual confounds. Teaches brain abscess vs GBM differentiation AND tumefactive MS vs GBM differentiation. Prior demyelinating history adds a sophisticated layer. DWI patterns, perfusion, and the necessity of tissue diagnosis are all emphasized. Penicillin allergy complicates empiric antibiotic selection if abscess were the diagnosis.

strengths: Dual confounds (brain abscess + tumefactive MS) create genuine diagnostic complexity
Prior optic neuritis history is a sophisticated distractor
Penicillin allergy adds treatment planning complexity
Well-differentiated from P01 despite similar dental history
